terms.definition: A relatively enduring self-concept that enables behavioral predictability across contexts
Limited in range and plasticity within an individual lifespan
Degree to which cognitive, emotional, and behavioral subsystems of a person produce non-contradictory outputs over time
Variance-adjusted stability of self-reported traits across domains
Subjective sense of initiating and controlling one’s own actions
Aggregate contextual demands forcing an individual to maintain multiple persona states simultaneously
Internal or communal demand to preserve an integrated, truthful self-representation
Loss of a privileged, non-performative vantage point from which the self can be coherently observed
A recursive monitoring construct that evaluates and optimises one’s outward persona performances
Measurable frequency or intensity of user interaction with a digital platform
Self-representation emergent from optimisation functions operating over engagement metrics
Capacity to align principle-based commitments with observable behaviour
Principle in quantum mechanics whereby the act of measurement alters the state of the system being measured
terms.first_use_section: Why Collapsed Observers Cannot See Reality
